Description: Controlled lab where only the logging mode varies, with trustworthy instrumentation.
Quantify the DNS performance penalty of native query logging vs dnstap.
Prove which option actually answers the security and operations questions.
Confirm always-on telemetry is safe and affordable at production scale.
The 3-year business case and the rollout plan.
Evidence-based, signed-off decision: native logging vs dnstap.

Summary: A1 Stand up POC lab and load generator
Description: Non-prod NIOS member + dedicated dnsperf/dnstrace generator host. AC: member identified/built; generator on a separate box; collector reachable; member->collector:6001 open; topology documented.

Summary: A2 Define load profiles and query mix
Description: AC: P1 steady, P2 peak, P3 bursty, P4 soak profiles with target QPS; query mix with cache-hit/miss(recursion)/NXDOMAIN and realistic qtype/qname; mix file in repo; identical replay across runs.

Summary: A3 Instrument member and collector resource capture
Description: AC: member CPU/mem/IO captured (node_exporter or NIOS ibPlatformOne SNMP); collector/Prometheus/Loki use captured; results sheet keyed by (test,mode,profile,run#).

Summary: B1 Measure baseline DNS performance (no logging)
Description: T1. AC: ramp QPS to SLA-break; record max sustainable QPS, p50/p95/p99 latency, member CPU/mem/IO; >=3 runs medians; baseline frozen as reference.
T2. AC: repeat all profiles with NIOS logging in 3 modes (queries; responses; both); report % QPS drop, latency delta, CPU/IO delta vs baseline; test Infoblox 'don't run both' guidance with data.
T3. AC: repeat all profiles with dnstap->collector on and native off; report QPS/latency/CPU deltas vs baseline; confirm events arrive during load.
T4. AC: charted native vs dnstap vs baseline at P2 peak; explicit penalty per option; which preserves more DNS headroom; feeds scorecard C1.

Summary: C1 Data completeness and fidelity comparison
Description: T5. AC: sample N queries per path; verify dnstap correlates query+response, shows recursion, captures client IP/qname/qtype/rcode/timing; document native gaps (response not bundled).

Summary: C2 Security and forensics query evaluation
Description: T10. AC: run threat-hunt queries on each dataset (tunneling via TXT/NULL/ANY volume, NXDOMAIN/DGA spikes, per-client domain history); record which option answers each; feeds scorecard C6.
T6. AC: P4 (2-4h) under native and dnstap; no CPU creep/memory leak/latency drift; collector keeps up; member stable.
T7. AC: under load kill collector and drop network; prove DNS resolution stays healthy; characterize dnstap buffer/drop and native disk-full/IO-stall behavior; document worst case.

Summary: D3 Storage volume and growth model
Description: T8. AC: bytes/event and rate at P1/P2 per option; project GB/day at prod QPS and storage for 30/90/365-day retention; note compression; feeds TCO and scorecard C5.
T9. AC: drive collector to event-rate ceiling; report max events/s before drop, Prometheus/Loki ingest headroom, Grafana responsiveness, host resource use; sizing + HA recommendation.
T11. AC: validate dnstap egress over LAN1 vs MGMT (out-of-band); confirm single rule DNS->collector:6001; confirm no data-plane coupling.

AC: tabulate appliance/headroom impact, licensing (incl Data Connector VM if modeled), storage ($/GB x retention x GB/day from D3), SIEM ingest, FTE/ops over 3y for native vs dnstap; credit dnstap deferred-upgrade avoidance; finance review.

Summary: E2 Implementation and day-2 operations plan
Description: AC: phased per-member rollout via InfoBlox Ops playbook; collector HA/sizing; retention policy; SIEM integration; alerting+dashboards; telemetry-host monitoring; change-window/restart guidance; rollback.

Summary: E3 Privacy compliance and security review
Description: AC: assess DNS-data sensitivity, retention limits, access control, encryption in transit, regulatory constraints for both options; Security/Compliance sign-off.

Summary: F1 Complete scorecard and weighted analysis
Description: AC: every scorecard cell filled from measured evidence; weighted totals per option; sensitivity noted (SIEM ingest cost, peak-QPS assumptions).
AC: written recommendation per decision framework with evidence; presented to DDI+Security+Ops; decision recorded; if dnstap E2 becomes rollout plan, if native codify on-demand guardrails.
